name: Chamonix-Mont-Blanc
slopeNotes: Separate ski areas linked by ski bus; very little true ski-in/ski-out. Grands Montets (Argentière) top ~3275m glacier terrain; Aiguille du Midi 3842m for Vallée Blanche (off-piste). Brévent gondola walkable from center.
licenseNotes: Zone tendue. Since 1 May 2025: change-of-use authorization required for renting second homes as meublés de tourisme; quota of 1 authorization per natural person in Chamonix & Les Houches; new PLU aims to ban new second-home construction. Buying here to short-term rent is effectively blocked unless the property already has rights or you make it a primary residence. Verified 2026-07.
rentalNotes: Exceptional dual-season demand BUT license quota makes new STR effectively unavailable — rental_yield must be discounted by license risk. — verify rates
notes: Included mainly as benchmark; license regime likely disqualifies it for this project's investment goal despite perfect mountain credentials.

name: La Clusaz
slopeNotes: Lifts from village center; some true ski-in/ski-out on Beauregard/Crêt du Merle sides. Top at Balme 2477m — the high, snow-sure sector. Linked ski bus to Le Grand-Bornand (Aravis pass).
licenseNotes: No adopted quota/change-of-use identified as of mid-2026 — verify quarterly; commune under strong second-home pressure (>70% second homes), a likely candidate to use Le Meur tools eventually. National registration applies.
rentalNotes: Closest 'real village' big resort to Geneva; strong weekend market. — verify rates
notes: Prices high (€8-10k/m²) — budget likely buys an apartment or a mazot/farmhouse outside center. Consider Manigod/Saint-Jean-de-Sixt as cheaper satellites (add later).

name: Saint-Gervais-les-Bains
slopeNotes: Gondola from town center to Le Bettex; true ski-in/ski-out at Le Bettex hamlet (1400m). Big linked domain (Evasion Mont-Blanc ~400km) but modest top altitude (Mont Joly sector ~2350m — verify exact). Tramway du Mont-Blanc to Les Houches side.
licenseNotes: No change-of-use quota adopted as of mid-2026 — verify quarterly; commune has discussed regulation of meublés. National registration (May 2026) applies. Classified tourist commune: 120-day primary-residence cap can be lowered to 90.
rentalNotes: Strong dual season: ski + thermal baths + TMB hikers. — verify rates
notes: Village at 810m but Saint-Nicolas-de-Véroce / Le Bettex sectors at 1200-1450m are the interesting micro-locations (farmhouses with views).

name: Sainte-Foy-Tarentaise
slopeNotes: Old village (1050m) is 10min drive below the purpose-built-but-tasteful station at 1550m (ski-in/ski-out there). Top Col de l'Aiguille 2620m, mostly north-facing. Small area (~40km pistes) but Val d'Isère/Tignes 25min, Les Arcs/La Rosière 20-30min.
licenseNotes: No Le Meur restrictions identified as of mid-2026 — verify quarterly. National registration applies. Tarentaise communes outside the big names have so far not restricted.
rentalNotes: Winter-skewed market; summer demand thin vs Haute-Savoie. Ski-in/ski-out at 1550 rents well to UK/NL market. — verify rates
notes: Tarentaise scout: tests the 2h+ drive tradeoff. Also gateway to adding Villaroger, Peisey, Champagny, Bourg-Saint-Maurice later.

name: Samoëns
resort: Grand Massif (Samoëns / Flaine / Morillon / Les Carroz / Sixt)
slopeNotes: Grand Massif Express gondola ~1km from village center (ski bus); top at Grandes Platières (Flaine) 2480m. 265km linked domain. Snow at village level unreliable (720m) but skiing is mid/high mountain.
licenseNotes: No change-of-use/quota regime identified as of mid-2026 — verify quarterly. National Le Meur registration applies. Commune historically pro-tourism.
rentalNotes: Solid family ski + strong summer (Fer-à-Cheval). — verify rates
notes: Arguably the best charm/access/price balance in Haute-Savoie; 2480m top is below the 2700m climate bar though — Flaine bowl is north-facing and holds snow.
